intro: Nototriche clandestina, also known as "Clandestine Nototriche," is a plant species native to South America, belonging to the Malvaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Nototriche clandestina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Nototriche clandestina is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and it may also be used in the form of tinctures or extracts to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits.
